exemplar.title:
To what extent can Mozambican journalists effectively investigate
human rights concerns and hold the government accountable?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent can Mozambican journalists effectively investigate
human rights concerns and hold the government accountable?

strengths: Clear and effective introduction situating the topic with recent reports and a concise roadmap
Research question stated verbatim and integrated throughout the plan
Methodology section justifies choice of contrasting interviewees, showing informed source selection
weaknesses: Minor lapses in phrasing and some repetition in topic explanation
Research question remains fairly broad without clear parameters
Methodology omits ethical considerations and discussion of potential biases
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates the topic accurately with a strong roadmap and explanation (5â6 band), but minor phrasing lapses prevent a perfect score. The research question is clear and linked to discussion but too broad for the 5â6 band. The methodology is mostly complete with relevant sources but lacks discussion of limitations, placing it in the upper half of the 3â4 band overall. A holistic score of 5 reflects strong performance with room for tightening focus and adding methodological rigor.

strengths: Clear and coherent knowledge of Mozambiqueâs history and conflict context
Effective integration of theoretical frameworks like Galtungâs Conflict Triangle
Accurate and consistent use of key terminology (e.g., structural violence, universalism)
weaknesses: Source application is often descriptive with incomplete citations
Sparse academic literature and occasional conflation of perspectives (civil society vs international)
Some in-text citations lack full details, reducing transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (knowledge of topic and use of terminology) sit firmly at the top of the 3â4 band, demonstrating coherent knowledge and consistent terminology. Application of source material shows relevance but lacks critical depth and complete citations, placing it at the lower end of the 3â4 band. The overall holistic score of 4 aligns with this mixed performance.

strengths: Good variety of primary and secondary evidence consistently linked to the research question
Nuanced argument structure using local, regional, and international lenses
Insightful international comparison (Mozambique vs Sri Lanka) elevating analysis
weaknesses: Sections occasionally revert to description with partially supported causal claims
Evaluation of evidence and recommendations lack sustained critical depth
Discussion omits exploration of alternative explanations regarding causation vs correlation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are placed in the 4â6 band. Research quality is solid and relevant; analysis shows good use of theory but sometimes descriptive; discussion is reasoned but unevenly critical. The work demonstrates good critical thinking overall but falls short of the 7â9 bandâs sustained depth. A holistic score of 6 (upper half of 4â6) appropriately rewards strengths while reflecting areas for deeper evaluation.

strengths: Clear, conventional structure with title page, contents, introduction, methodology, body, conclusion, and works cited
Headings and subheadings effectively support navigation and understanding
General adherence to layout conventions including page numbering and consistent typography
weaknesses: Several entries in the table of contents lack page numbers
Minor inconsistencies in citation formatting and typographic clutter
An incomplete sentence in the background section disrupts flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure scores at the top of the 3â4 band with clear organization and navigability. Layout also fits the 3â4 band despite minor inconsistencies. Overall, the presentation is professional and coherent, warranting the maximum band score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Analysis moves beyond narration by linking events to Galtungâs triangle and relativism/universalism debates and by comparing Mozambique with Sri Lanka.  However, sections often revert to description and causal claims (e.g., press freedom and poverty) are asserted rather than systematically evidenced.  Conclusions are partially supported, placing the work in the middle of the 4-6 band.

The investigation assembles a good variety of primary and secondary evidence that remains largely pertinent to the RQ.  Although academic depth could be greater, the research is mostly relevant, triangulates international and local perspectives, and underpins later argumentation, justifying a mid-band (4-6) score.

The essay follows a conventional EE structure with title page, contents, introduction, methodology, thematically organised body, conclusion and works cited.  Headings and sub-headings aid navigation and the flow is logical, so the structure actively supports understanding, warranting the top of the 3-4 band.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of Mozambiqueâs post-colonial history, constitutional provisions and recent conflict, and integrates Global Politics concepts (structural violence, cultural relativism).  While depth is uneven across sections, the overall narrative is coherent and sources are generally used effectively, warranting the upper level of the 3-4 band.

The student presents multiple perspectives (government, civil society, international) and acknowledges limitations (difficulty accessing local journalists).  Evaluation of evidence is present but uneven, and recommendations are brief.  The discussion is reasoned yet lacks sustained critical depth, aligning with a solid but not outstanding performance within the 4-6 band.

Layout conventions (title, word count, table of contents, in-text citations, works cited) are observed and page numbers are indicated in the ToC.  Minor inconsistencies in citation formatting and some typographic clutter slightly detract from professionalism but overall requirements are met correctly, meriting a secure 3 within the 3-4 band.

Terminology such as ânegative peace,â âstructural violence,â âuniversalism,â and âsovereigntyâ is employed accurately and consistently.  Occasional typographical errors do not obscure meaning.  Usage is therefore mostly accurate and appropriate, placing the work at the top of the 3-4 band.

The student clearly states the focus on âJournalistic Liberty in Mozambique â¦â and immediately situates the investigation within recent Amnesty and RSF reports.  Background, scope (corruption + Cabo Delgado) and intended structure are explicitly laid out, demonstrating accurate and effective communication.  Minor lapses in phrasing and some repetition mean the explanation is strong rather than fully exemplary, justifying a score at the lower end of the 5-6 band.

A methodology section is provided, explaining the choice of two interviews and a broad range of NGO/press sources.  While selection is partly justified (international vs local perspectives), the range is weighted toward advocacy websites and newspapers, and there is limited discussion of source limitations or triangulation.  This represents a mostly complete but not fully rigorous methodology, meriting the upper half of the 3-4 band.

The research question is stated verbatim and is linked to the planned discussion throughout.  However, it remains fairly broad (combining âeffectively investigateâ with âhold the government accountableâ across multiple sub-topics) and the wording does not confine parameters such as time frame or specific indicators of âeffectiveness.â  Connection to the discussion is adequate but not sharply delimited, placing it solidly in the 3-4 band.

The essay cites numerous specific cases, reports and statistics, and references are embedded in the narrative.  Nonetheless, source use is often descriptive, some citations are incomplete, and academic literature is sparse.  Application is generally relevant but not always closely analysed, fitting the lower end of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The detailed description of investigative journalism milestones in Mozambique demonstrates effective application of diverse sources.
Several entries in the table of contents lack page numbers for key sections, hindering navigation and clarity. Please complete all page references.
The table of contents is neatly formatted with consistent typography, supporting reader orientation effectively.
In-text citations for the 2023 and 2024 Amnesty and RSF reports lack full details; add author, date, and page for each to enhance source transparency.
The introduction includes a concise roadmap of the essayâs structure, effectively orienting the reader to the investigation plan.
The closing sentence in the background section is incomplete, truncating the thought on enforcement. Revise to ensure clarity and coherence.
The historical context of Mozambiqueâs post-colonial period is well-researched and demonstrates clear disciplinary knowledge.
The account of the 2017 Cabo Delgado conflict succinctly relates to the research focus, showing appropriate selection of background detail.
The methodology description effectively justifies the choice of two contrasting interviewees, illustrating informed source selection.
The methodology omits discussion of ethical considerations and potential biases in interviews; include reflection on these to strengthen rigor.
The narrative on Carlos Cardoso provides rich case evidence but jumps between years; restructure chronologically for clearer analysis.
Statistics and examples in the Cabo Delgado section demonstrate effective source application, supporting the investigationâs depth.
Augment the Cabo Delgado evidence with local NGO or academic data to triangulate Human Rights Watch claims and strengthen research quality.
The breakdown into local, regional, and international lenses shows nuanced argument development and effective evaluation.
The selected examples of violence against journalists are varied and wellâdocumented, bolstering the investigationâs research quality.
The list of violent incidents would benefit from a brief analytical summary linking them to the research questionâs focus on accountability.
The international comparison with Sri Lanka is insightful and elevates the analytical depth of the argument.
The perspectives section conflates civil society and international voices. Distinguish these viewpoints more clearly for stronger critical evaluation.
The integration of Galtungâs Conflict Triangle provides a robust theoretical framework to analyze journalistic risks.
The anecdote about Dr. Hanlon is descriptive; contrast this qualitative insight with quantitative data on journalist arrests for stronger analysis.
The reflection is mostly descriptive; deepen the analysis by evaluating how methodological choices impacted findings.
The juxtaposition of cultural relativism and universalism demonstrates sophisticated theoretical understanding.
The discussion on press freedomâs link to development omits consideration of correlation versus causation; address potential alternative explanations.
The conclusion restates the findings clearly but introduces new comparative material that was not fully developed earlier; remove or integrate this content.
The conclusionâs recommendations are pertinent but could be prioritized and expanded with concrete action steps.
The reflection on methodology acknowledges limitations effectively, linking them to the broader research finding.
The reference list covers a wide range of primary and secondary sources, illustrating extensive research.
HTML artifacts like â\bulletâ appear in list items, distracting the reader. Please remove inline math and use standard bullet points.
The title is clearly stated at the start, satisfying the requirement for immediate identification of the investigationâs focus.
The word count is provided and falls within the 4,000-word limit, demonstrating compliance with formatting requirements.
Citation formatting in the background on Carlos Cardoso is inconsistent (e.g., missing italics and date placement). Standardize to a single style.
The definition of cultural violence from Galtung requires a precise citation from the IBDP course guide; please add the original source reference.
Separate duplicated entries across multiple pages in the reference list; consolidate to avoid redundancy and streamline presentation.
URL lineâbreaks in the reference entries disrupt readability; ensure web addresses remain unbroken or use correct hyphenation rules.
Alphabetize the Works Cited entries consistently to improve navigability and conform to standard practice.
The Works Cited demonstrates breadth but shows inconsistency in citation style and formatting across entries.
The entry for the E-International Relations article lacks author date and full title; include complete bibliographic details.
